title: Algebra P1: Document hex-core ports as a Σ-algebra
rationale: Phase 1 of ADR-2604111229 — write docs/algebra/ports-signature.md enumerating all 10 hex-core port traits as an algebraic signature. This is the foundation the other algebra documents will reference. Documentation-only — no code changes except .gitignore to track docs/algebra/.

steps.description: Update .gitignore to allow-list docs/algebra/ (mirrors the existing pattern for docs/adrs, docs/analysis, etc.)
steps.done_condition: git status shows new files under docs/algebra/ as untracked (not ignored)

steps.description: Extract signatures from hex-core/src/ports/*.rs — for each of the 10 port traits, read the file and record: trait name, operation list, each operation's argument types and result type, error type, and layer stratum (domain / ports / usecases / adapters allowed to invoke).
steps.done_condition: All 10 ports read: file_system, inference, coordination, enforcement, agent_runtime, brain, build, context_compressor, sandbox, secret

steps.description: Write docs/algebra/ports-signature.md. Structure: (1) Overview explaining the free Σ-algebra view, (2) one section per port with operations as bulleted signatures, (3) 'The interpreter' section explaining the composition root as a Σ-algebra morphism into IO, (4) 'Hexagonal rules as theorems' section translating each existing rule into a statement over Σ-subalgebras, (5) 'Known gaps' section flagging current code that diverges from the algebra. Target: under 1000 lines.
steps.done_condition: docs/algebra/ports-signature.md exists, < 1000 lines, has all 5 structural sections, covers all 10 ports

steps.description: Cross-reference from CLAUDE.md and the ports mod.rs — add a one-line pointer from CLAUDE.md's 'Hexagonal Architecture Rules' section to docs/algebra/ports-signature.md, and add a doc comment at the top of hex-core/src/ports/mod.rs pointing readers at the algebraic spec.
steps.done_condition: grep 'ports-signature' CLAUDE.md → 1 hit; grep 'ports-signature' hex-core/src/ports/mod.rs → 1 hit

steps.description: Run hex readme validate to confirm no regression (the doc additions should not touch any tracked counts). Run cargo test -p hex-cli --lib commands::readme to confirm.
steps.done_condition: hex readme validate reports 0 errors; cargo test passes

successCriteria: docs/algebra/ports-signature.md exists and covers all 10 port traits
CLAUDE.md and hex-core/src/ports/mod.rs both cross-reference the new doc
hex readme validate still reports 0 errors
ADR-2604111229 phase P1 can be marked 'Done' in follow-up commit

riskRegister.risk: Document drifts from code the first time a new port is added without updating the doc
riskRegister.mitigation: Phase P2 (separate workplan) adds an automated check in hex readme validate that every trait in hex-core/src/ports/*.rs appears as a section in the algebra doc

riskRegister.risk: Readers find the formal notation intimidating and skip the doc
riskRegister.mitigation: Lead every section with prose; put formal notation in 'Formal statement' subsections; include a worked example per port
